content.full_text:
February 8, 1977

Clay M. Johnston  
California Lathing and Plastering Contracting Association, Inc.  
9911 Inglewood Avenue  
Suite 205  
Inglewood, California 90301

Dear Mr. Johnston:

Thank you for your letter of January 24, 1977, in which you ask the question whether a registered lobbyist may attend and purchase a ticket for a retirement banquet being given to a state agency director. Since it is unclear from your letter as to the identity of the registered lobbyist, the answer I am providing you may not be used as a defense in any action brought by a law enforcement agency. However, I am sure the following advice will be useful to you and the lobbyist.

The Political Reform Act does not prohibit a registered lobbyist from attending a banquet in honor of a state agency official, nor does it prevent the lobbyist from purchasing a ticket for the event provided that in the latter instance the proceeds of the ticket given to the state official are not more than $10. (Government Code Section 86203) This section also prohibits the lobbyist from arranging or acting as an intermediary for any gift of more than $10 per official. Thus, the lobbyist may not arrange for any other person (such as a spouse or other lobbyist) to attend the dinner if the cost of such arrangement causes the total amount of the proceeds to the official to be over $10.

If you have any further questions, please feel free to call me.

Sincerely,

Robert M. Stern  
General Counsel

sections.conclusion: The Political Reform Act does not prohibit a registered lobbyist from attending a banquet in honor of a state agency official, nor does it prevent the lobbyist from purchasing a ticket for the event provided that in the latter instance the proceeds of the ticket given to the state official are not more than $10. Thus, the lobbyist may not arrange for any other person (such as a spouse or other lobbyist) to attend the dinner if the cost of such arrangement causes the total amount of the proceeds to the official to be over $10.
sections.question_synthetic: May a registered lobbyist attend and purchase a ticket for a retirement banquet honoring a state agency director under the Political Reform Act?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: A registered lobbyist may attend and purchase a banquet ticket honoring a state agency official, provided the ticket proceeds to the official do not exceed $10 under Government Code Section 86203. The lobbyist cannot arrange for others to attend or act as an intermediary for gifts exceeding $10 per official.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Clear advice letter with question and conclusion sections. Minor caveat noted by counsel regarding uncertain identity of lobbyist.

embedding.summary: FPPC General Counsel advises that a registered lobbyist may attend a retirement banquet for a state agency director and purchase a ticket, provided the proceeds to the official do not exceed $10, and that the lobbyist cannot arrange for others to attend or facilitate gifts exceeding this limit.
